intake.topic_summary: Mk.XII Phase 3b 70B-class clean evidence path BLOCKED via RunPod (gemma-27b ~40GB cumulative ceiling 4-strike + runpod-pre-ssh sustained 4-reproduction). Strategy: alt-vendor routing matrix — vast.ai (A100/H100 marketplace) + AWS (EC2 G5/G6 H100) + GCP (A2/A3 H100) + Lambda Labs / CoreWeave / Fluidstack. Each vendor: cost vs bandwidth vs setup-effort tradeoff.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: vast-ai-h100-cumulative-bytes-no-ceiling
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: vast.ai H100 pod download 40GB+ without platform termination — falsifies if SAME ~35-40GB cumulative ceiling appears
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: vast.ai H100 spawn + Llama-3.1-70B fp16 download attempt

falsifiers_preregistered.name: aws-ec2-g6-h100-pre-ssh-no-stuck
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: AWS EC2 G6 H100 SSH-ready < 5min — falsifies if AWS reproduces RunPod's pre-SSH stuck pattern
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: AWS EC2 spawn + ssh keypair + boot time

falsifiers_preregistered.name: multi-pod-persistent-volume-relay-feasibility
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: RunPod persistent network volume + 2-pod relay (download pod + measurement pod with same volume) bypasses cumulative-bytes ceiling — falsifies if volume share fails OR ceiling applies per-volume
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: RunPod persistent volume create + 2-pod attach test (no model load, just file write/read)
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: volume mounts both pods AND 50GB+ write+read PASS

falsifiers_preregistered.name: 70B-int4-unsloth-on-alt-vendor-fits-40GB-pod
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: 70B unsloth int4 quantized = ~40GB total weights → fits within H100 80GB single-pod with int4 measurement (no relay needed) on alt-vendor
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: alt-vendor H100 pod + Llama-3.1-70B unsloth int4 download + bnb GPU pre-flight + forward

falsifiers_preregistered.name: alt-vendor-cost-vs-runpod-baseline
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: Alt vendor H100 hourly cost competitive with RunPod ($2.99/hr secureCloud baseline). vast.ai $1-2/hr expected, AWS G6 H100 ~$5-8/hr. F5 falsifies if alt-vendor avg cost > 1.5x RunPod baseline.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: vendor pricing pages + spot vs on-demand vs reserved

axis_a.name: alt-vendor routing matrix (vast.ai / AWS / GCP / Lambda Labs / CoreWeave)
axis_a.saturation_evidence: 5+ alt vendors identified, decision matrix built. vast.ai = primary cost-optimized + AWS/GCP = reliability backup. RunPod = blocked.
axis_a.limit_witness_pointing_to: categorical-limit (5+ vendor universe) + cost-saturation (vast.ai $1-2/hr lower bound at marketplace level)

axis_b.name: cumulative-bytes ~40GB ceiling bypass strategies

bypass_strategies.name: int4_quantized_under_ceiling
bypass_strategies.description: 70B unsloth int4 = ~40GB total — fits within ceiling threshold. Single-pod path.
bypass_strategies.applicability: single-pod measurement, raw#10 caveat: int4 vs fp16 regime mixing

bypass_strategies.name: persistent_volume_2_pod_relay
bypass_strategies.description: Pod A: download 70B fp16 to persistent volume. Pod B: attach volume + measure. Each pod cumulative < ceiling.

bypass_strategies.name: alt_vendor_no_ceiling
bypass_strategies.description: Migrate to vast.ai/AWS/GCP — no known cumulative-bytes ceiling. Direct fp16 path.
bypass_strategies.applicability: primary recommended, depends on F1 falsifier verdict

bypass_strategies.name: multi_shard_chunked_resume
bypass_strategies.description: Use snapshot_download with explicit shard-by-shard resume; pause between shards to avoid burst patterns.
bypass_strategies.applicability: RunPod-only mitigation, may not bypass true cumulative ceiling

bypass_strategies.name: preflight_estimate_abort
bypass_strategies.description: Pattern 6b — pre-flight estimate_total_download_gb() abort if > 35GB; route to multi-pod or alt-vendor early

axis_b.saturation_evidence: 5 bypass strategies identified covering full mitigation surface (quantization + relay + alt-vendor + chunking + pre-flight)
axis_b.limit_witness_pointing_to: algorithmic-bound (5 mitigation strategies = full mitigation surface for cumulative-bytes problem)

axis_c.name: multi-pod relay architecture for large model measurement

relay_components.responsibility: long-running, no measurement deadline, fetches model to persistent volume / local disk + verifies integrity
relay_components.lifetime: minutes to hours, no SSH-ready strict deadline

relay_components.responsibility: attach volume / scp from R1, run forward + tomography measurement, write results
verify result JSON schema + checksum + raw 91 honesty triad post-emission
Mac-local nexus_remote dispatch, lifecycle management, watchdog, audit ledger emission per raw 86

axis_c.architecture_topology: linear pipeline R1 → R2 → R3 with R4 supervising. Cross-vendor: R1 on vast.ai (cheap long-running), R2 on AWS (reliable measurement), R3 on hetzner (CPU-only verification).
axis_c.saturation_evidence: 4-component pipeline covers full lifecycle (download / measure / verify / orchestrate) with clear separation of concerns
axis_c.limit_witness_pointing_to: categorical-limit (4 roles minimum for relay; reduction below 4 collapses concerns) + computational-limit (no further decomposition without losing atomicity)

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis a (vendor matrix) vs axis b (bypass strategies) ≈ 0.30 (vendor B3 strategy depends on alt-vendor presence). axis a vs c (vendor) ≈ 0.20 (relay can span vendors). axis b vs c ≈ 0.40 (B2 persistent volume relay = subset of axis c R1+R2). All < 0.7 raw 48 PASS but b-c moderate overlap acknowledged.
